## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(unternehmensname, ''))) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('gold', 'golden') THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('silver', 'silber') THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('bronze') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('platinum') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(industrie, ''))) AS "Industry",
    CASE
        WHEN homepage IS NOT NULL AND homepage !~ '^https?://' THEN 'http://' || TRIM(homepage)
        ELSE TRIM(homepage)
    END AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(stadt, ''))) AS "BillingCity",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(land_region, ''))) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- kundennummer: Deterministic hash (MD5) of the source customer number to ensure consistency across all tables referencing AccountId.
  - Name <- unternehmensname: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_nr: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source ERP number.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- kundenklasse: Mapped from source customer class to the enum domain (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum). Case-insensitive matching with NULL for unparseable values.
  - Region__c <- vertriebsgebiet: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source sales region.
  - Industry <- industrie: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Website <- homepage: Trimmed and prefixed with 'http://' if missing a scheme (http:// or https://).
  - BillingCity <- stadt: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - BillingCountry <- land_region: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- kundennummer: Directly mapped from the source customer number for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Defaulted to 0 (false) for all rows.
Reasoning: The model transforms the `master_kunden` table into the `Account` target schema. The `Id` is generated as a deterministic MD5 hash of `kundennummer` to ensure consistency across all tables referencing `AccountId`. Enum fields like `Customer_Tier__c` are mapped to their respective domains with case-insensitive matching. Text fields are trimmed and normalized to title case where applicable. The `Website` field is ensured to start with a scheme (http:// or https://). Static defaults are used for `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted`.

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH customer_accounts AS (
    SELECT 
        kundennummer,
        MD5(kundennummer) AS account_id
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
)

SELECT 
    MD5(Trim(k.kontakt_id)) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.rufname)) AS "FirstName",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.familienname)) AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(k.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(k.tel) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER', 'END USER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('DEUTSCH', 'DE', 'GERMAN') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('ENGLISH', 'ENGLISCH', 'EN') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('FRANZÖSISCH', 'FRENCH', 'FR') THEN 'FR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('SPANISCH', 'SPANISH', 'ES') THEN 'ES'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('ITALIENISCH', 'ITALIAN', 'IT') THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    ca.account_id AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(k.kontakt_id) AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kontakte') }} k
LEFT JOIN customer_accounts ca ON TRIM(k.kd_nummer) = ca.kundennummer
```

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH asset_data AS (
    SELECT
        ma.asset_kennung,
        ma.asset_name,
        ma.serien_nummer,
        ma.garantieende,
        ma.kunden_kennung,
        ma.projekt_kennung,
        mk.kundennummer,
        mp.projekt_kennung AS projekt_kennung_join
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} ma
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON ma.kunden_kennung = mk.kundennummer
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} mp
        ON ma.projekt_kennung = mp.projekt_kennung
)

SELECT
    MD5(asset_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Account__c",
    MD5(projekt_kennung) AS "Project__c",
    asset_kennung AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM asset_data
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- master_assets.asset_kennung: Deterministic hash (MD5) of the source asset_kennung to generate a Salesforce-style Id.
  - Name <- master_assets.asset_name: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP.
  - Serial_Number__c <- master_assets.serien_nummer: Trimmed and passed through as-is.
  - Warranty_End_Date__c <- master_assets.garantieende: Parsed from multiple date formats (DD.MM.YYYY, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD) and output as ISO YYYY-MM-DD. NULL for unparseable values.
  - Account__c <- master_kunden.kundennummer: Joined via master_assets.kunden_kennung = master_kunden.kundennummer and hashed (MD5) to generate a Salesforce-style Account__c.
  - Project__c <- master_projekte.projekt_kennung: Joined via master_assets.projekt_kennung = master_projekte.projekt_kennung and hashed (MD5) to generate a Salesforce-style Project__c.
  - Legacy_Asset_ID__c <- master_assets.asset_kennung: Directly mapped from the source asset_kennung.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default to 0 (false) for all rows.
Reasoning: The model joins master_assets with master_kunden and master_projekte to resolve Account__c and Project__c as deterministic hashes of their respective source keys. ## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_data AS (
    SELECT
        mo.opp_kennung,
        mo.titel,
        mo.vertriebsphase,
        mo.zieldatum,
        mo.auftragswert,
        mo.waehrungscode,
        mo.kunden_ref,
        mk.kundennummer
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} mo
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON REPLACE(mo.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = mk.kundennummer
)

SELECT
    MD5(od.opp_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(od.titel)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('prospecting', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('qualification', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('needs analysis', 'bedarfsanalyse') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('value proposition', 'wertargumentation') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'entscheider identifiziert') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('perception analysis', 'wahrnehmungsanalyse') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'verhandlung') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN od.zieldatum
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN od.auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(od.auftragswert) = 'None' THEN NULL
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        ELSE 
            CASE
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE NULL
            END
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN od.waehrungscode IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.waehrungscode, '[^A-Za-z]', '', 'g')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE
        WHEN od.kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(od.kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "AccountId",
    od.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_data od
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- master_opportunities.opp_kennung: MD5 hash of the source opportunity key `opp_kennung` to generate a Salesforce-style Id.

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.

- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.

Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.

Output schema for dbt model results: `rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2`
Output tables to inspect (quoted exactly): "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Account", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Contact", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Opportunity", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Project__c", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Installed_Asset__c".
Use query_postgres to inspect tables in that schema after dbt has run.

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(unternehmensname, ''))) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('gold', 'golden') THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('silver', 'silber') THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('bronze') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('platinum') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(industrie, ''))) AS "Industry",
    CASE
        WHEN homepage IS NOT NULL AND homepage !~ '^https?://' THEN 'http://' || TRIM(homepage)
        ELSE TRIM(homepage)
    END AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(stadt, ''))) AS "BillingCity",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(land_region, ''))) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- kundennummer: Deterministic hash (MD5) of the source customer number to ensure consistency across all tables referencing AccountId.
  - Name <- unternehmensname: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_nr: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source ERP number.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- kundenklasse: Mapped from source customer class to the enum domain (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum). Case-insensitive matching with NULL for unparseable values.
  - Region__c <- vertriebsgebiet: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source sales region.
  - Industry <- industrie: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Website <- homepage: Trimmed and prefixed with 'http://' if missing a scheme (http:// or https://).

WITH asset_data AS (

        ma.asset_kennung,
        ma.asset_name,
        ma.serien_nummer,
        ma.garantieende,
        ma.kunden_kennung,
        ma.projekt_kennung,
        mk.kundennummer,
        mp.projekt_kennung AS projekt_kennung_join
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} ma

    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN

    END AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Account__c",
    MD5(projekt_kennung) AS "Project__c",
    asset_kennung AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",

FROM asset_data

## Opportunity
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH opportunity_data AS (
    SELECT
        mo.opp_kennung,
        mo.titel,
        mo.vertriebsphase,
        mo.zieldatum,
        mo.auftragswert,
        mo.waehrungscode,
        mo.kunden_ref,
        mk.kundennummer
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} mo
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON REPLACE(mo.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = mk.kundennummer
)

SELECT
    MD5(od.opp_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(od.titel)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('prospecting', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('qualification', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('needs analysis', 'bedarfsanalyse') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('value proposition', 'wertargumentation') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'entscheider identifiziert') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('perception analysis', 'wahrnehmungsanalyse') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'verhandlung') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN od.zieldatum
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN od.auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(od.auftragswert) = 'None' THEN NULL
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        ELSE 
            CASE
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE NULL
            END
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN od.waehrungscode IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.waehrungscode, '[^A-Za-z]', '', 'g')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE
        WHEN od.kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(od.kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "AccountId",
    od.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_data od
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- master_opportunities.opp_kennung: MD5 hash of the source opportunity key `opp_kennung` to generate a Salesforce-style Id.
  - Name <- master_opportunities.titel: Trimmed and title-cased version of `titel`.
  - StageName <- master_opportunities.vertriebsphase: Mapped from `vertriebsphase` to the target enum values (case-insensitive). Fallback to NULL for unparseable values.
  - CloseDate <- master_opportunities.zieldatum: Parsed from `zieldatum` (handling DD.MM.YYYY, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD formats) and output as ISO YYYY-MM-DD. NULL for unparseable.
  - Amount <- master_opportunities.auftragswert: Stripped of currency symbols and locale-specific separators (e.g., EUR 1.234,56 → 1234.56). Cast to DOUBLE PRECISION. NULL for unparseable or 'None'.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- master_opportunities.waehrungscode: Trimmed and uppercased `waehrungscode` with non-alphabetic characters removed.
  - AccountId <- master_opportunities.kunden_ref, master_kunden.kundennummer: MD5 hash of `kundennummer` from `master_kunden`, joined via `kunden_ref` (with KD- → CUST- prefix replacement). NULL if no match.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- master_opportunities.opp_kennung: Directly mapped from `opp_kennung`.

  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z'.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default to 0 (false) for all rows.

WITH project_data AS (
    SELECT
        mp."projekt_kennung",
        mp."projektname",
        mp."projektstatus",
        mp."go_live_datum",
        mp."kunden_kennung",
        mp."opp_kennung_ref",
        mk."kundennummer" AS account_kundennummer,
        mo."opp_kennung" AS opportunity_opp_kennung
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} mp

        ON mp."kunden_kennung" = mk."kundennummer"
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} mo
        ON mp."opp_kennung_ref" = mo."opp_kennung"
)

SELECT
    MD5(p."projekt_kennung") AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(p."projektname")) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('ACTIVE', 'AKTIV') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('ABGESCHLOSSEN', 'COMPLETED') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('IN PLANUNG', 'PLANUNG', 'IN PLANNING') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('ON HOLD', 'HOLD') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('CANCELLED', 'STORNIERT') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{4}\d{2}\d{2}$' THEN
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p."go_live_datum", 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p."go_live_datum", 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p."go_live_datum", 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND p."go_live_datum" != '0000-00-00' THEN
            p."go_live_datum"
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    CASE WHEN p.account_kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(p.account_kundennummer) ELSE NULL END AS "Account__c",
    CASE WHEN p.opportunity_opp_kennung IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(p.opportunity_opp_kennung) ELSE NULL END AS "Opportunity__c",
    p."projekt_kennung" AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM project_data p

1. **`Name`**: The first row has an empty string (`""`) for `Name`. The target schema requires `Name` to be **NOT NULL**. This violates the constraint.

2. **`Website`**: URLs are correctly prefixed with `http://` or `https://` where missing.

3. **`Customer_Tier__c`**: Values are correctly mapped to the enum domain (`Gold`, `Silver`, `Bronze`).

4. **`Industry`**: Values like `"It"` and `"Industrie"` are title-cased (`INITCAP`), but some may need further normalization (e.g., `"It"` → `"IT"`).

5. **`BillingCountry`**: Some values like `"St. Pierre Und Miquelon"` and `"Malediven"` appear unusual but are not invalid.

6. **`Legacy_Customer_ID__c`**: Correctly populated from `kundennummer`.

---

Next, I will inspect the **Contact** table for:

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 5, 35, 58, 115853, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.


## fixture_master_v2_src.master_assets  (800 rows)

# Target schema

- `Account__c` text
- `Project__c` text
- `Legacy_Asset_ID__c` text
- `CreatedDate` text
- `LastModifiedDate` text
- `IsDeleted` integer

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Output schema for dbt model results: `rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2`
Output tables to inspect (quoted exactly): "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Account", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Contact", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Opportunity", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Project__c", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Installed_Asset__c".
Use query_postgres to inspect tables in that schema after dbt has run.

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(unternehmensname, ''))) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('gold', 'golden') THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('silver', 'silber') THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('bronze') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('platinum') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(industrie, ''))) AS "Industry",
    CASE
        WHEN homepage IS NOT NULL AND homepage !~ '^https?://' THEN 'http://' || TRIM(homepage)
        ELSE TRIM(homepage)
    END AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(stadt, ''))) AS "BillingCity",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(land_region, ''))) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- kundennummer: Deterministic hash (MD5) of the source customer number to ensure consistency across all tables referencing AccountId.
  - Name <- unternehmensname: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_nr: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source ERP number.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- kundenklasse: Mapped from source customer class to the enum domain (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum). Case-insensitive matching with NULL for unparseable values.
  - Region__c <- vertriebsgebiet: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source sales region.
  - Industry <- industrie: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Website <- homepage: Trimmed and prefixed with 'http://' if missing a scheme (http:// or https://).
  - BillingCity <- stadt: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - BillingCountry <- land_region: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- kundennummer: Directly mapped from the source customer number for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Defaulted to 0 (false) for all rows.
Reasoning: The model transforms the `master_kunden` table into the `Account` target schema. The `Id` is generated as a deterministic MD5 hash of `kundennummer` to ensure consistency across all tables referencing `AccountId`. Enum fields like `Customer_Tier__c` are mapped to their respective domains with case-insensitive matching. Text fields are trimmed and normalized to title case where applicable. The `Website` field is ensured to start with a scheme (http:// or https://). Static defaults are used for `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted`.

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH customer_accounts AS (
    SELECT 
        kundennummer,
        MD5(kundennummer) AS account_id
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
)

SELECT 
    MD5(Trim(k.kontakt_id)) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.rufname)) AS "FirstName",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.familienname)) AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(k.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(k.tel) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER', 'END USER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('DEUTSCH', 'DE', 'GERMAN') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('ENGLISH', 'ENGLISCH', 'EN') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('FRANZÖSISCH', 'FRENCH', 'FR') THEN 'FR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('SPANISCH', 'SPANISH', 'ES') THEN 'ES'

WITH asset_data AS (
    SELECT
        ma.asset_kennung,
        ma.asset_name,
        ma.serien_nummer,
        ma.garantieende,
        ma.kunden_kennung,
        ma.projekt_kennung,
        mk.kundennummer,
        mp.projekt_kennung AS projekt_kennung_join
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} ma
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON ma.kunden_kennung = mk.kundennummer

        ON ma.projekt_kennung = mp.projekt_kennung
)

SELECT
    MD5(asset_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Warranty_End_Date__c",
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Account__c",
    MD5(projekt_kennung) AS "Project__c",
    asset_kennung AS "Legacy_Asset_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",

FROM asset_data

## Opportunity
```sql

WITH opportunity_data AS (
    SELECT
        mo.opp_kennung,
        mo.titel,
        mo.vertriebsphase,
        mo.zieldatum,
        mo.auftragswert,
        mo.waehrungscode,
        mo.kunden_ref,
        mk.kundennummer
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} mo
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON REPLACE(mo.kunden_ref, 'KD-', 'CUST-') = mk.kundennummer
)

SELECT
    MD5(od.opp_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(od.titel)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('prospecting', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('qualification', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('needs analysis', 'bedarfsanalyse') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('value proposition', 'wertargumentation') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'entscheider identifiziert') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('perception analysis', 'wahrnehmungsanalyse') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'verhandlung') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN od.zieldatum
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN od.auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(od.auftragswert) = 'None' THEN NULL
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        ELSE 
            CASE
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE NULL
            END
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN od.waehrungscode IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.waehrungscode, '[^A-Za-z]', '', 'g')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE
        WHEN od.kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(od.kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "AccountId",
    od.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_data od
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- master_opportunities.opp_kennung: MD5 hash of the source opportunity key `opp_kennung` to generate a Salesforce-style Id.
  - Name <- master_opportunities.titel: Trimmed and title-cased version of `titel`.
  - StageName <- master_opportunities.vertriebsphase: Mapped from `vertriebsphase` to the target enum values (case-insensitive). Fallback to NULL for unparseable values.
  - CloseDate <- master_opportunities.zieldatum: Parsed from `zieldatum` (handling DD.MM.YYYY, YYYYMMDD, MM/DD/YYYY, YYYY-MM-DD formats) and output as ISO YYYY-MM-DD. NULL for unparseable.
  - Amount <- master_opportunities.auftragswert: Stripped of currency symbols and locale-specific separators (e.g., EUR 1.234,56 → 1234.56). Cast to DOUBLE PRECISION. NULL for unparseable or 'None'.
  - CurrencyIsoCode <- master_opportunities.waehrungscode: Trimmed and uppercased `waehrungscode` with non-alphabetic characters removed.
  - AccountId <- master_opportunities.kunden_ref, master_kunden.kundennummer: MD5 hash of `kundennummer` from `master_kunden`, joined via `kunden_ref` (with KD- → CUST- prefix replacement). NULL if no match.
  - Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c <- master_opportunities.opp_kennung: Directly mapped from `opp_kennung`.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z'.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z'.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default to 0 (false) for all rows.
Reasoning: The model joins `master_opportunities` with `master_kunden` using a prefix-replaced `kunden_ref` (KD- → CUST-) to resolve `AccountId`. It handles multiple date and amount formats, maps `vertriebsphase` to the target enum, and ensures NOT NULL constraints with defaults.

```sql

WITH project_data AS (
    SELECT
        mp."projekt_kennung",
        mp."projektname",
        mp."projektstatus",
        mp."go_live_datum",
        mp."kunden_kennung",
        mp."opp_kennung_ref",
        mk."kundennummer" AS account_kundennummer,
        mo."opp_kennung" AS opportunity_opp_kennung
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} mp
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON mp."kunden_kennung" = mk."kundennummer"
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_opportunities') }} mo
        ON mp."opp_kennung_ref" = mo."opp_kennung"
)

SELECT
    MD5(p."projekt_kennung") AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(p."projektname")) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('ACTIVE', 'AKTIV') THEN 'Active'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('ABGESCHLOSSEN', 'COMPLETED') THEN 'Completed'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('IN PLANUNG', 'PLANUNG', 'IN PLANNING') THEN 'In Planning'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('ON HOLD', 'HOLD') THEN 'On Hold'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(p."projektstatus")) IN ('CANCELLED', 'STORNIERT') THEN 'Cancelled'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Project_Status__c",
    CASE
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{4}\d{2}\d{2}$' THEN
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p."go_live_datum", 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p."go_live_datum", 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{1,2}\.\d{1,2}\.\d{4}$' THEN
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(p."go_live_datum", 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN TRIM(p."go_live_datum") ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' AND p."go_live_datum" != '0000-00-00' THEN
            p."go_live_datum"
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Go_Live_Date__c",
    CASE WHEN p.account_kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(p.account_kundennummer) ELSE NULL END AS "Account__c",
    CASE WHEN p.opportunity_opp_kennung IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(p.opportunity_opp_kennung) ELSE NULL END AS "Opportunity__c",
    p."projekt_kennung" AS "Legacy_Project_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM project_data p
```

  - Name <- master_projekte.projektname: Normalized with INITCAP and TRIM for consistent casing.
  - Project_Status__c <- master_projekte.projektstatus: Mapped from source `projektstatus` to the enum values (Active, Completed, In Planning, On Hold, Cancelled). Null for unparseable values.

  - Opportunity__c <- master_opportunities.opp_kennung: Joined via `opp_kennung_ref = opp_kennung` and hashed with MD5 to generate the Salesforce-style Opportunity Id.
  - Legacy_Project_ID__c <- master_projekte.projekt_kennung: Directly mapped from the source natural key `projekt_kennung`.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Defaulted to 0 (false) for all rows.

args.sql:
SELECT 
  "Id", 
  "Name", 
  "StageName", 
  "CloseDate", 
  "Amount", 
  "CurrencyIsoCode", 
  "AccountId", 
  "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c", 
  "CreatedDate", 
  "LastModifiedDate", 
  "IsDeleted"
FROM "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Opportunity" 
LIMIT 10;

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 5, 35, 58, 115853, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

dbt source slug: `fixture_master_v2_src` — use this exact string as the first argument to `source()` in dbt models.
`query_postgres` uses plain PostgreSQL only (no `{{ source(...) }}`): e.g. `SELECT * FROM "fixture_master_v2_src"."master_assets" LIMIT 10`.

# Target schema


## Account

## Project__c

Columns:

- `Serial_Number__c` text

SELECT
    MD5(od.opp_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(od.titel)) AS "Name",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('prospecting', 'in kontakt') THEN 'Prospecting'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('qualification', 'quali') THEN 'Qualification'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('needs analysis', 'bedarfsanalyse') THEN 'Needs Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('value proposition', 'wertargumentation') THEN 'Value Proposition'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('id. decision makers', 'entscheider identifiziert') THEN 'Id. Decision Makers'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('perception analysis', 'wahrnehmungsanalyse') THEN 'Perception Analysis'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('proposal/price quote', 'angebot') THEN 'Proposal/Price Quote'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('negotiation/review', 'verhandlung') THEN 'Negotiation/Review'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed won', 'abgeschlossen (gewonnen)', 'closed won') THEN 'Closed Won'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(od.vertriebsphase)) IN ('closed lost', 'abgeschlossen (verloren)', 'lost') THEN 'Closed Lost'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "StageName",
    CASE
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN od.zieldatum
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{8}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'YYYYMMDD'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN od.zieldatum ~ '^\d{1,2}/\d{1,2}/\d{4}$' THEN TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(od.zieldatum, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "CloseDate",
    CASE
        WHEN od.auftragswert IS NULL OR TRIM(od.auftragswert) = 'None' THEN NULL
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(REPLACE(od.auftragswert, ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^[0-9]+$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        WHEN od.auftragswert ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
            CAST(od.auftragswert AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
        ELSE 
            CASE
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3},[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), '.', ''), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                WHEN REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g') ~ '^-?[0-9]+,[0-9]{2}$' THEN 
                    CAST(REPLACE(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.auftragswert, '[^0-9.,-]', '', 'g'), ',', '.') AS DOUBLE PRECISION)
                ELSE NULL
            END
    END AS "Amount",
    CASE
        WHEN od.waehrungscode IS NULL THEN NULL
        ELSE UPPER(TRIM(REGEXP_REPLACE(od.waehrungscode, '[^A-Za-z]', '', 'g')))
    END AS "CurrencyIsoCode",
    CASE
        WHEN od.kundennummer IS NOT NULL THEN MD5(od.kundennummer)
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "AccountId",
    od.opp_kennung AS "Legacy_Opportunity_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"
FROM opportunity_data od

ModelRequest(parts=[SystemPromptPart(content='You are a meticulous staff data engineer reviewing a dbt mapping proposal.

Your task:
1. Review the dbt SQL models for correctness, PostgreSQL validity, and adherence to the target schema
2. Use the query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect the OUTPUT of the dbt models by querying the output schema
3. Verify data quality: row counts, join correctness, NULL handling, enum values, data types
4. Check for cross-table consistency issues

When querying output tables, quote schema and table names exactly (case-sensitive), e.g. `"my_out_schema"."Account"`.

You have access to:
- The full dbt SQL proposal with column notes and reasoning
- query_postgres(sql) tool to inspect both source and output data

Return a structured CritiqueReport with one TableVerdict per proposed table:
- verdict "approved" when the model is correct as written
- verdict "needs_revision" with concrete, actionable comments otherwise (cite specific data issues found via queries)

Do NOT request stylistic changes. Only flag real correctness or data quality problems.

## PostgreSQL SQL rules

- Use only PostgreSQL syntax. Do NOT use TRY_CAST, SAFE_CAST, ISNULL, NVL, or invented functions/macros.
- Model output must be executable SQL only — no markdown fences, prose, filenames, or non-ASCII commentary outside string literals.
- Quoted identifiers: PostgreSQL folds unquoted identifiers to lowercase. For mixed-case target column names, always alias with double quotes, e.g. `expr AS "ColumnName"` or `expr AS "Custom_Field__c"`.
- CAST syntax: `CAST(expr AS type)` requires the `AS type` clause. Inner CASE branches that already cast to the target type do not need a redundant outer CAST without `AS type`.
- To coerce text to integer when digits-only, use: `CASE WHEN col ~ '^\d+$' THEN col::INTEGER ELSE NULL END`
- Use regex (`~`) and REGEXP_REPLACE for text cleanup before casting to numeric types.

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.

## query_postgres tool rules

- `query_postgres(sql)` runs plain PostgreSQL against staging — NOT dbt. Never pass `{{ source(...) }}` or other Jinja to query_postgres.
- Use quoted schema and table names, e.g. `SELECT * FROM "my_source_schema"."my_table" LIMIT 10`.
- When introspecting output tables, quote mixed-case schema, table, and column names exactly as materialized.', timestamp=datetime.datetime(2026, 7, 8, 5, 35, 58, 115853, tzinfo=datetime.timezone.utc)), UserPromptPart(content='# Source database

You MUST produce exactly one dbt model per target table (5 total): Account, Contact, Opportunity, Project__c, Installed_Asset__c.

# Transformation guidelines

## Transformation rules

- Output column names and types must match the target spec exactly. Use double-quoted aliases for mixed-case names (see PostgreSQL rules).
- Model file body must be executable SQL only — no wrapper functions, markdown fences, or prose outside SQL comments.
- Populate every `Legacy_*__c` column from the source natural key (e.g. customer number, contact id). These legacy columns are used to verify row-level correctness.
- `AccountId` and `Account__c` must reference the Salesforce-style Account `Id`, not raw source customer numbers. Join or map source keys when formats differ.
- For enum-typed target columns, map source values into the declared enum domain. Normalize case and spelling. Apply a consistent fallback policy (NULL vs a sensible default) across all tables.
- Dates: accept multiple text formats in source data; output consistent ISO `YYYY-MM-DD` text where applicable. Prefer NULL over sentinel dates when source is missing or unparseable. Never cast raw date strings directly — parse with `TO_DATE`/`TO_CHAR` or regex guards first (e.g. `DD.MM.YYYY`, `YYYYMMDD`, `MM/DD/YYYY`).
- Amounts and currencies: strip currency symbols and text prefixes before casting. Handle locale-specific separators explicitly (e.g. European `1.234,56` vs `1234.56`). For dot+comma European values, remove thousand-separator dots then swap comma to decimal point before `::DOUBLE PRECISION`.
- Cross-table keys: use the same key transform everywhere a foreign key appears. Inspect sample rows or use query_postgres to detect prefix or format mismatches between related source columns — do not assume join columns match literally.
- NOT NULL target columns: satisfy constraints with meaningful defaults, not empty strings unless that is intentional.
- Use INITCAP / LOWER / TRIM for normalisation where the target spec or source data quality suggests it.


Output schema for dbt model results: `rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2`
Output tables to inspect (quoted exactly): "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Account", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Contact", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Opportunity", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Project__c", "rtyjsc824_plan_write_critique_master_v2"."Installed_Asset__c".
Use query_postgres to inspect tables in that schema after dbt has run.

# Proposed mapping

## Account
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

SELECT
    MD5(kundennummer) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(unternehmensname, ''))) AS "Name",
    TRIM(erp_nr) AS "ERP_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('gold', 'golden') THEN 'Gold'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('silver', 'silber') THEN 'Silver'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('bronze') THEN 'Bronze'
        WHEN LOWER(TRIM(kundenklasse)) IN ('platinum') THEN 'Platinum'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Customer_Tier__c",
    TRIM(vertriebsgebiet) AS "Region__c",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(industrie, ''))) AS "Industry",
    CASE
        WHEN homepage IS NOT NULL AND homepage !~ '^https?://' THEN 'http://' || TRIM(homepage)
        ELSE TRIM(homepage)
    END AS "Website",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(stadt, ''))) AS "BillingCity",
    INITCAP(TRIM(COALESCE(land_region, ''))) AS "BillingCountry",
    kundennummer AS "Legacy_Customer_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- kundennummer: Deterministic hash (MD5) of the source customer number to ensure consistency across all tables referencing AccountId.
  - Name <- unternehmensname: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - ERP_Number__c <- erp_nr: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source ERP number.
  - Customer_Tier__c <- kundenklasse: Mapped from source customer class to the enum domain (Gold, Silver, Bronze, Platinum). Case-insensitive matching with NULL for unparseable values.
  - Region__c <- vertriebsgebiet: Trimmed and passed through as-is from the source sales region.
  - Industry <- industrie: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Website <- homepage: Trimmed and prefixed with 'http://' if missing a scheme (http:// or https://).
  - BillingCity <- stadt: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - BillingCountry <- land_region: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP. Defaults to empty string if NULL.
  - Legacy_Customer_ID__c <- kundennummer: Directly mapped from the source customer number for row-level verification.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp for all rows.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Defaulted to 0 (false) for all rows.
Reasoning: The model transforms the `master_kunden` table into the `Account` target schema. The `Id` is generated as a deterministic MD5 hash of `kundennummer` to ensure consistency across all tables referencing `AccountId`. Enum fields like `Customer_Tier__c` are mapped to their respective domains with case-insensitive matching. Text fields are trimmed and normalized to title case where applicable. The `Website` field is ensured to start with a scheme (http:// or https://). Static defaults are used for `CreatedDate`, `LastModifiedDate`, and `IsDeleted`.

## Contact
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH customer_accounts AS (
    SELECT 
        kundennummer,
        MD5(kundennummer) AS account_id
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }}
)

SELECT 
    MD5(Trim(k.kontakt_id)) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.rufname)) AS "FirstName",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.familienname)) AS "LastName",
    LOWER(TRIM(k.kontakt_email)) AS "Email",
    TRIM(k.tel) AS "Phone",
    INITCAP(TRIM(k.berufsbezeichnung)) AS "Title",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('DECISION MAKER', 'ENTSCHEIDER') THEN 'Decision Maker'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('END USER', 'ENDANWENDER', 'END USER') THEN 'End User'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('TECHNICAL CONTACT', 'TECHNISCHER ANSPRECHPARTNER') THEN 'Technical Contact'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.rolle)) IN ('EXECUTIVE SPONSOR') THEN 'Executive Sponsor'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Role__c",
    CASE 
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('DEUTSCH', 'DE', 'GERMAN') THEN 'DE'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('ENGLISH', 'ENGLISCH', 'EN') THEN 'EN'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('FRANZÖSISCH', 'FRENCH', 'FR') THEN 'FR'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('SPANISCH', 'SPANISH', 'ES') THEN 'ES'
        WHEN UPPER(TRIM(k.korrespondenzsprache)) IN ('ITALIENISCH', 'ITALIAN', 'IT') THEN 'IT'
        ELSE NULL
    END AS "Preferred_Language__c",
    ca.account_id AS "AccountId",
    TRIM(k.kontakt_id) AS "Legacy_Contact_ID__c",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "CreatedDate",
    '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z' AS "LastModifiedDate",
    0 AS "IsDeleted"

FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kontakte') }} k
LEFT JOIN customer_accounts ca ON TRIM(k.kd_nummer) = ca.kundennummer
```
Column notes:
  - Id <- master_kontakte.kontakt_id: MD5 hash of kontakt_id to generate a Salesforce-style Id.
  - FirstName <- master_kontakte.rufname: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP.
  - LastName <- master_kontakte.familienname: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP.
  - Email <- master_kontakte.kontakt_email: Trimmed and converted to lowercase.
  - Phone <- master_kontakte.tel: Trimmed and passed through as-is.
  - Title <- master_kontakte.berufsbezeichnung: Trimmed and normalized to title case using INITCAP.
  - Role__c <- master_kontakte.rolle: Mapped to enum values: Decision Maker, End User, Technical Contact, Executive Sponsor. Unparseable values set to NULL.
  - Preferred_Language__c <- master_kontakte.korrespondenzsprache: Mapped to enum values: DE, EN, FR, ES, IT. Unparseable values set to NULL.
  - AccountId <- master_kunden.kundennummer: MD5 hash of kundennummer from master_kunden to generate Salesforce-style AccountId.
  - Legacy_Contact_ID__c <- master_kontakte.kontakt_id: Directly mapped from kontakt_id.
  - CreatedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z'.
  - LastModifiedDate <- (none): Static default timestamp '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z'.
  - IsDeleted <- (none): Default value 0 (false).
Reasoning: The model joins `master_kontakte` with `master_kunden` to resolve the `AccountId` as an MD5 hash of `kundennummer`. All text fields are trimmed and normalized (INITCAP for names/titles, lowercase for emails). Enum fields (`Role__c`, `Preferred_Language__c`) are mapped from source values to their respective domains. Static defaults are used for timestamps and `IsDeleted`.

## Installed_Asset__c
```sql
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

WITH asset_data AS (
    SELECT
        ma.asset_kennung,
        ma.asset_name,
        ma.serien_nummer,
        ma.garantieende,
        ma.kunden_kennung,
        ma.projekt_kennung,
        mk.kundennummer,
        mp.projekt_kennung AS projekt_kennung_join
    FROM {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_assets') }} ma
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_kunden') }} mk
        ON ma.kunden_kennung = mk.kundennummer
    LEFT JOIN {{ source('fixture_master_v2_src', 'master_projekte') }} mp
        ON ma.projekt_kennung = mp.projekt_kennung
)

SELECT
    MD5(asset_kennung) AS "Id",
    INITCAP(TRIM(asset_name)) AS "Name",
    TRIM(serien_nummer) AS "Serial_Number__c",
    CASE
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$' THEN garantieende
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}\.\d{2}\.\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'DD.MM.YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
        WHEN garantieende ~ '^\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}$' THEN 
            TO_CHAR(TO_DATE(garantieende, 'MM/DD/YYYY'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
